behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is the CLI’s interaction with real `git` rename detection/output (`R... old -> new`) plus report aggregation. A mocked git response can hide flag/format mismatches that only appear with real subprocess output. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git/CLI classification, with no AI/ML or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary handling failures commonly occur at real subprocess/stdout decoding and real filesystem content boundaries. In-process fakes often miss decode/format edge cases from actual `git diff` on binary blobs. Rejected higher levels: behavior text involves git diff parsing only, not AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI process, assert exit code is success and binary-related counts are present.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git behavior in an unborn-HEAD repo (actual exit codes/messages), which is a subprocess boundary. Mocking can hide the exact no-commit failure mode. Rejected higher levels: no model-dependent behavior in description.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI process, assert successful execution and an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, requiring real commits and real git query semantics. Stubbed history can miss command/format mismatches and revision-range mistakes. Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic git-history accounting, not AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI, and assert report counts equal the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Git index vs working-tree semantics are real VCS state transitions at filesystem/subprocess boundaries. Mocks can miss differences in command options and index handling. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create identical content changes once unstaged and once staged, run CLI in each state, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output for equivalent changes.
